fields.helpText: This field contains the word ONE, and can only contain this word.  Also, there can only be one entry in this file.
This field denotes the device which is assigned to print all of the nursing exception reports.  This printer should be on at all times.
This switch will allow the site manager to bring the Nursing application  off-line without bringing the entire system down.

fields.setOfCodes: 1:ACCESS TO NEW PERSON FILE;0:NO ACCESS TO NEW PERSON FILE;
fields.helpText: This field allows the Site Manager to turn on/off access to the NEW PERSON file from the Nursing Package.
This switch is used by the NURSINIT's ONLY.  NO EDITING ALLOWED!!!
This switch is used by the NURAINIT's ONLY.  NO EDITING ALLOWED!!!
This switch is used by the NURQINIT's ONLY.  NO EDITING ALLOWED!!!
This switch is used by the NURCINIT's ONLY.  NO EDITING ALLOWED!!!
This field will contain the last date that the Nursing Acuity/ Separation-Activation Run has run.  This is the Nursing Acuity/ Separation-Activation Run that calculates patient category totals.
Flag denoting whether or not the Nursing Acuity/Separation-Activation Run ran to completion.

The ward that was last being processed by the Nursing Acuity/ Separation-Activation Run.  This number is the variable NWARD in the routine NURAAU0, and corresponds to an internal entry number in the NURS Location file.

The patient that was last being processed by the Nursing Acuity/ Separation-Activation Run.  This number is the variable DFN in the routine NURAAU0, and it corresponds to the internal entry number in the Patient file.
Flag denoting whether or not the midnight batch job ran to completion. being processed by the midnight acuity batch job.

The ward that was last being processed for midnights by the Nursing Acuity/ Separation-Activation Run.  This number is the variable NWARD in the routine NURAMU0, and corresponds to an internal entry number in the NURS Location file.

The patient that was last being processed for midnights by the Nursing Acuity/Separation-Activation Run.  This number is the variable DFN in the routine NURAMU0, and it corresponds to the internal entry number in the Patient file.
This field indicates whether or not the Nursing Acuity/Separation- Activation Run has been completed.
Flag denoting whether or not the the Nursing Acuity/Separation-Activation Run ran to completion.

The ward that was last being processed by the Nursing Acuity/Separation- Activation Run.  This number is the variable NWARD in the routine NURAMB1, and corresponds to an internal entry number in the NURS Location File.

The patient that was last being processed by the Nursing Acuity/Separation- Activation Run.  This number is the variable DFN in the routine NURAMB1, and it corresponds to the internal entry number in the patient file.
The time that the acuity totals will be calculated for the day shift for the current workload statistics report.
The time that the acuity totals will be calculated for the evening shift for the current workload statistics report.
The time that the acuity totals will be calculated for the night shift for the AMIS and current workload statistics report.

This field defaults the percentage of professional personnel assigned to a unit if the professional percentage field is not defined in the NURS Location file.  This data is used in the calculation of unit workload statistics.
This field contains the status of the Nursing Acuity/Separation-Activation Run for a specific date.
This field indicates the version of the Nursing Service General Module (NURS) software.
The field indicates the version of the Nursing Administration (NURA)  Package.
This field indicates the version of the Nursing Education (NURE) Package.
This field indicates the version of the Nursing Clinical (NURC) Package.

The data in this field will allow the site to change the title of the report generated by the Patient Plan of Care, Print (NURCPP-CARE) option. (NURCPP-CARE) option.  For example, the title, Nursing Care Plan, can be renamed to Patient Care Plan.

The data in this field allows the site to change the header, Patient Problem, for the report generated by the Patient Plan of Care, Print (NURCPP-CARE) option.  If this field has no data, the Patient Problem heading will still print.

The data in this field allows the site to change the Goals/Expected Outcomes heading on the report generated by the Patient Plan of Care, Print (NURCPP-CARE) option.  If this field has no data then the heading Goals/ Expected Outcomes will be used. will be used.

The data in this field allows the site to change the Nursing Interventions/ Orders heading of the report generated by the Patient Plan of Care, Print (NURCPP-CARE) option.  If this field has no data, then the Nursing Interventions/Orders heading will be used. Interventions/Orders heading will be used.
The data in this field represents the default evaluation date displayed in the Patient Plan of Care, Edit (NURCPE-CARE) option when an evaluation date for a problem is edited. edited.
The data in this field represents the default target date displayed in the Patient Plan of Care, Edit (NURCPE-CARE) option. (NURCPE-CARE) option when an target date for a goal is edited.

fields.fieldName: PRINT ATT NUMS ON EOS RPT
fields.helpText: This flag will control whether or not the attending physician numbers  will print on the Nursing End of Shift Report.

This field indicates whether or not FTEE data in the NURS Position Control file (#211.8) is associated with multiple services/product lines. 'YES' indicates that an FTEE can be associated with one of multiple services or product lines. 'NO' indicates that all FTEE is associated with Nursing Service.

This field indicates whether or not the facilty is multi-divisional. YES indicates that each nursing location may be associated with a separate and different division/institution.  NO indicates that all locations are associated with a single institution.
This field contains the ZTSK number of the Automated MAR Pharmacy Extract Batch Job.

This field will contain a number if the patient acuity job is currently being calculated. If the NURAAM-ACU (tasked) option is running the number will be the task number. If the NURAAM-ACUMAN (manual) option is running the number will be '1'.
